question.text: Extension of tenure of a close-ended AIF may be permitted up to:

answer.text: 2 years with approval of two-thirds of unit holders by value

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Extension of tenure may be permitted up to 2 years, subject to approval of two-thirds of unit holders by value of their investment. For Accredited Investor only funds, extension can be up to 5 years with similar approval. **The Trap:** The two-thirds requirement is by value of investment, not by number of unit holders. A few large investors could block an extension if they hold more than one-third by value. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tenure extension provisions are tested in XIX-A and XIX-C. The different limits for regular AIFs (2 years) vs AI-only funds (5 years) are a recent distinction. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Inform investors that tenure extensions are possible but require supermajority consent. If their investment is small relative to the fund, they may not have blocking power on extension decisions.
